steps.title: Use the scale factor
steps.content: Count the relevant scale factor before setting up the equation. If side lengths are multiplied by a factor, areas are multiplied by the square of that factor.

steps.title: Identify the linear scale factor
steps.content: Square A has side lengths that are 72 times those of square B. The linear scale factor is 72.

steps.title: Apply the area scaling rule
steps.content:
Using the relationship between linear and area scale factors:
$$k = 72^2 = 5184$$
That matches the correct answer - A is correct.
